rulings.Case Title: JULES RADCLIFFE et al VS. PHUONG PHAM et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 30, 2014, line 2. DEFENDANT TP PHAM, LLC DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT: SUSTAINED with leave to amend to allege the terms of contact and whether contact was oral, written or implied as to each Plaintiff; to allege facts supporting each element of the cause of action for violation of SFRO 37.9. Opposing party and its counsel is ordered to comply with CRC 2.108 and 3.113 in future. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Pltf'S Verified Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One, Doc Requests, Set One, And Special Interrogatories, Set One; Request For Monetary And/Or Terminating Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 30, 2014, line 5. DEFENDANT SOJOURN PROPERTIES INC., STEVEN KING's Motion to Compel Plaintiff's Verified Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One, Document Requests, Set One, And Special Interrogatories, Set One; Request For Monetary And/Or Terminating Sanctions: Continued to May 8, 2014 to be heard in Department 302 at 9:00 a.m. by judge pro tem. Opposing party shall provide a courtesy copy of opposition to department 302 no later than April 30, 2014 by 4:00 p.m. with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. All exhibits must be separated by exhibit tab. CRC3.1110(f). = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: DIANA D. SAM VS. CATALINA BOYKIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action Pending Completion Of Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 30, 2014, line 6. PLAINTIFF DIANA SAM's Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action Pending Completion Of Arbitration: DENIED. Motion is premature as there is a scheduled mediation, Plaintiff waived its right to compel arbitration by actively participating in the litigation; not all parties can be compelled to arbitrate. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: VALERIE LEE VS. MICHAEL W. SPENCER et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 30, 2014, line 9. DEFENDANT MICHAEL SPENCER's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT: DENIED. This unlawful detainer is based on a "3 Day Notice" which asserts two separate just causes under Rent Ordinance Even though moving party shifted the burden as to the SFRO 37.9(a)(7) (See Michal W. Spencer Declaration), burden is not shifted as to SFRO 37.9(a)(2) as no evidence is presented regarding landlord's unreasonable refusal of a written request to add Michael A. Spencer to the unit. Also, moving party's argument that Michael A. Spencer is an original occupant is not supported by facts or legal authorities: (1) Motion is based in part on records on file with the Court (See Notice of Motion 2:2) .The record in this case indicates that (a) Michael A. Spencer was an occupant as of 1995 (See Ex. 1 to First Amended Complaint; reference to "Mike" in 5); (b) Michael A. Spencer was not an occupant as of 2/18/11 (See Ex. 1. to Plaintiff's Declaration filed on March 28, 2014); (c) Michael A. Spencer was an occupant at least as of December 12, 2013 when "3 Day Notice" was served. Moving party provides no declaration stating that Michael A. Spencer maintained uninterrupted occupancy since 1995 and moving party cites no authority for the proposition that the status of "original occupant" is maintained by a person who leaves and then returns to the premises. Michael W. Spencer has not filed an answer. CCP 1170.7. To the extent defendant seeks reconsideration of April 2, 2014 Order, it is denied as improperly made in Reply filed on April 24, 2014. Also, waiver has not been alleged as affirmative defense by Michael A. Spencer in his answer on file. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: VALERIE LEE VS. MICHAEL W. SPENCER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Void Default Inproperly Entered
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 30, 2014, line 9. DEFENDANTS MICHAEL SPENCER, MICHAEL SPENCER Motion To Vacate Void Default Inproperly Entered: GRANTED. Filing of an affidavit in support of motion for summary judgment is considered a general appearance. See Wilson v. Barry (1951) 102 Cal.App.2d 778. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CALLIE GREEN VS. JAMIE JAMES et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 30, 2014, line 10. DEFENDANTS JAMIE JAMES, DEBRA LEMMONS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT: OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Notice is fatally defective as to non-payment of rent. However, allegations of nuisance and illegal activity in the Notice/complaint support a cause of action for unlawful detainer. = (501/REQ)
